name: Platform / infrastructure / DevOps tooling
summary: Infrastructure and tooling that other engineering teams build and deploy on: CI/CD pipelines, IaC, Kubernetes operators/controllers, and self-service control planes. High-blast-radius, multi-tenant, privileged automation whose failures cascade to everything it hosts.

concerns: multi-tenant isolation between teams/workloads: namespace/network-policy/quota boundaries so one tenant cannot reach another's resources
least-privilege by default: scoped RBAC/IAM, no standing admin, short-lived credentials, and no ambient high-privilege pipeline access to prod
secrets management: external secret store, encryption, rotation, no secrets in Git/logs/images
software supply-chain security: signed artifacts (Sigstore), provenance/attestation (SLSA), SBOM generation, and admission policy that rejects unsigned/unscanned images
policy-as-code guardrails (OPA/Gatekeeper/Kyverno) enforcing security baselines on every change
audit trail of all privileged/control-plane actions (who changed what, when)
questions: Is tenant isolation specified so no team's workload can reach another's resources, and is access least-privilege with no standing admin?
Are supply-chain controls (artifact signing, SLSA provenance, SBOM, admission of only signed/scanned images) required?
Are secrets externalized/rotated (never in Git/logs) and privileged control-plane actions fully audited?

concerns: declarative, reproducible infrastructure (IaC/GitOps): desired state in version control, drift detection and reconciliation, no manual snowflake changes
upgrade, rollback, and blast-radius control: staged/canary rollouts across environments, controlled promotion, and safe rollback of infra and platform components
self-service with guardrails: golden paths/templates that are secure-by-default while limiting what tenants can do
disaster recovery for the platform itself: backup/restore of control-plane state (etcd, IaC state), RTO/RPO, and tested recovery runbooks
environment/config management across dev/stage/prod and multiple clusters/regions/clouds
questions: Is infrastructure declarative/reproducible (IaC/GitOps) with drift detection and no manual changes?
Are upgrade/rollback, staged rollout, and blast-radius controls specified for platform changes?
Is disaster recovery of the control-plane state (backup/restore, RTO/RPO, tested runbooks) defined?

concerns: control-plane availability and self-healing: reconciliation loops converge and recover without operator intervention
failures must degrade gracefully — a platform outage should not take down already-running tenant workloads (control-plane/data-plane separation)
idempotent, safely-retryable reconciliation (operators/pipelines must converge, not thrash, on repeat)
resilience of shared dependencies (registry, state backend, secret store) and their SLOs
questions: Does a control-plane outage leave already-running tenant workloads unaffected (control/data-plane separation)?
Are reconciliation loops idempotent and self-healing (converge, not thrash)?
Are availability targets for the platform and its shared dependencies defined?

concerns: platform observability: metrics/logs/traces for pipelines, controllers, and tenant activity, plus golden signals and SLO dashboards
testability of infra changes: plan/dry-run/diff, policy tests, and ephemeral preview environments before apply
versioning of platform APIs/CRDs and modules; deprecation of platform capabilities without breaking tenants
modular, reusable modules/charts/templates maintained centrally so fixes propagate
questions: Are pipelines/controllers observable (metrics/logs/traces, golden signals)?
Are infra changes testable via plan/dry-run/diff and policy tests before apply?
Are platform API/CRD versioning and non-breaking deprecation for tenants specified?

concerns: multi-cloud / multi-cluster / hybrid target support and avoidance of hard provider lock-in where required
environment parity so the same declarative definition deploys across dev/stage/prod and regions
packaging/distribution of the tooling itself (operator images, CLI, provider plugins) and its install/upgrade path
abstraction over heterogeneous infrastructure backends
questions: Are the target clouds/clusters/environments and any lock-in constraints specified?
Is environment parity (same definition across dev/stage/prod/regions) required?
Are packaging and install/upgrade of the platform tooling itself defined?

concerns: compatibility with the underlying platform API versions (e.g. Kubernetes version skew, deprecated API removal) and provider/API versions
interoperability with the existing toolchain (VCS, registries, identity providers, ticketing, scanners)
CRD/API contract stability for tenants and integrating systems
questions: Are supported platform/provider API versions and version-skew/deprecation handling specified?
Is interoperability with the existing toolchain (VCS, registry, IdP, scanners) defined?

concerns: mandated infrastructure stack, approved clouds/regions, and prohibited services/technologies
open-source licensing of bundled provider plugins/modules/base images (copyleft exposure in distributed tooling)
cost/budget ceilings and FinOps guardrails enforced by the platform
questions: Are mandated/approved infrastructure, clouds, and prohibited technologies stated?
Are OSS licensing constraints on bundled modules/images/plugins addressed?
Are cost/budget guardrails captured as requirements?

concerns: provisioning/reconciliation latency and pipeline throughput (build/deploy time, queue wait) at team scale
control-plane scalability: number of tenants/objects/CRDs reconciled and API-server/controller load
concurrency of pipeline runners and resource-quota headroom under peak demand
questions: Are provisioning/reconciliation latency and pipeline throughput targets quantified?
Is control-plane scalability (tenant/object counts, controller load) specified?
Is behaviour under peak concurrent pipeline demand defined?
